zotero.abstractNote: Approximate policy iteration (API) algorithms are a cornerstone of reinforcement learning. They alternate between policy evaluation and policy improvement. In the approximate setting, both steps are typically performed by solving a regression problem. However, the policy evaluation step often suffers from the projection error, which can lead to unstable learning or divergence. In this paper, we propose a new projection operator for API algorithms that is based on the idea of minimizing the Bellman error in a weighted norm. We show that our approach is equivalent to a weighted least-squares problem and that it can be solved efficiently. We provide theoretical guarantees for our approach and demonstrate its effectiveness on a set of benchmark problems.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 36th International Conference on Machine Learning
